name: basic-patch-data
description: External memory patching: change TestTarget's stack-local `value` variable from 600 to 9999 using WriteProcessMemory from a separate process. No code injection, no new thread or DLL inside the target. The expected outcome with the current ETW provider list is an empty trace — the kernel-process provider does not surface cross-process memory writes. That absence is itself the finding.

extra.notes: Writes 0F270000 (= 9999 as little-endian int32) into TestTarget's `value` variable. Press SPACE after the run to observe value=10000, confirming the patch landed. --verify reads the bytes back to confirm the write.
